behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff: must prove both auth rejection and no enqueue side effect. A pure Unit test of HMAC comparison would miss accidental forwarding. Real OS/network is not required (in-process HTTP harness + fake queue is enough), and no AI/ML is involved. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires 401 + no forward, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook handler with an in-process request/response harness using a real raw body and mismatched X-Signature-256, with a spy/fake queue writer module wired in. Assert HTTP 401 and assert queue write was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue adapter). Unit is too low because the bug appears at module interaction/order. System is not strictly required if persistence is exercised via local in-process storage adapter in test; no external network/subprocess is specified. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to the real queue module (or local persistence-backed queue adapter) and instrument completion points. Send a valid webhook and assert queue write commit/resolve happens before response end/200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is coupling between enqueue/ack path and downstream worker invocation. Needs module wiring, not just one function. Real OS/network/subprocess is unnecessary; a controlled slow downstream promise in-process reproduces the failure. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server/handler wired to a downstream processor stub that blocks (e.g., unresolved promise for several seconds). Send a valid event and assert response is returned promptly while downstream task remains unfinished at response time.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly wall-clock SLA under concurrency pressure, which depends on real runtime scheduling, request handling, and timing effects that mocks often hide. This crosses into real process/HTTP timing behavior, so System is the minimum robust boundary. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process, generate concurrent prior events that keep downstream workers busy, then send a new valid webhook over real HTTP and measure elapsed wall-clock to response. Assert response completes within 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at boundary between handler, idempotency store/check, and queue write path. Unit tests of a dedup helper can miss real handoff/order bugs. Real external infra is not required if dedup store and queue are wired in-process (or local DB). No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with real dedup logic and queue adapter. Send the same event ID twice (including near-concurrent submissions). Assert first request enqueues once and second does not produce an additional queue write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is mapping a queue-layer full signal to HTTP semantics. Needs handler+queue interaction; Unit is too low if signal propagation is miswired. Higher level rejected: text says 'when the queue reports it is full' (signal handling), not capacity enforcement itself; real OS/network is unnecessary here. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler with queue module stub/fake that deterministically returns a 'full' error code. Send a valid request and assert HTTP 503 plus Retry-After header value/presence.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Target is real queue implementation behavior (not mocked), requiring queue modules + persistence layer together. That is beyond Unit. System was considered, but behavior text does not require external network service or subprocess queue; a local persistence-backed queue integration test can expose real capacity bugs. No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity using local test storage, enqueue up to limit, then attempt one additional enqueue. Assert overflow enqueue is rejected and prior accepted entries remain intact.
